stem: Find the volume of the solid bounded below by the plane \(z=0\), above by the plane \( z = [[p]]x + [[q]]y \), whose base is the triangle \( 0 \le y \le x,\ 0 \le x \le [[a]] \), by evaluating \( \int_{0}^{[[a]]}\int_{0}^{x}\int_{0}^{[[p]]x+[[q]]y} 1\,dz\,dy\,dx \).
solution: The innermost integral gives the height \( \int_{0}^{[[p]]x+[[q]]y}1\,dz = [[p]]x+[[q]]y \). Then \( \int_{0}^{[[a]]}\int_{0}^{x}([[p]]x+[[q]]y)\,dy\,dx = \int_{0}^{[[a]]}\left([[p]]x^2 + \tfrac{[[q]]}{2}x^2\right)dx = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This integrates \(y\) over \([0,[[a]]]\) (a rectangular base) instead of the triangle \(0 \le y \le x\).
This makes a sign error on the \(y\)-term after integrating.
This is off by one from the correct value.
This is off by one from the correct value.
This is off by two from the correct value.

stem: Find the volume of the tetrahedron bounded by the coordinate planes \(x=0\), \(y=0\), \(z=0\) and the plane \( \dfrac{x}{[[A]]} + \dfrac{y}{[[B]]} + \dfrac{z}{[[C]]} = 1 \), by setting up and evaluating the appropriate triple integral in Cartesian coordinates.
solution: For fixed \(x\), \(y\) ranges from \(0\) to \([[B]](1-x/[[A]])\) (where the plane meets \(z=0\)), and for fixed \(x,y\), \(z\) ranges from \(0\) to \([[C]](1-x/[[A]]-y/[[B]])\). Evaluating \( \int_0^{[[A]]}\int_0^{[[B]](1-x/[[A]])}\int_0^{[[C]](1-x/[[A]]-y/[[B]])} 1\,dz\,dy\,dx \) gives the classic tetrahedron-volume formula \( \dfrac{[[A]]\cdot[[B]]\cdot[[C]]}{6} = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This is the volume of the full rectangular box with these side lengths, forgetting the \(\tfrac16\) factor for a tetrahedron.
This uses \(\tfrac12\), the factor for a triangular PRISM, instead of \(\tfrac16\) for a tetrahedron.
This uses \(\tfrac13\), the factor for a cone or pyramid with a full rectangular base, instead of \(\tfrac16\).
This is off by one from the correct value.
This is off by one from the correct value.

stem: Find the volume of the solid bounded below by \(z=0\), above by the plane \( z = [[p]]x + [[q]]y + [[r]] \), over the RECTANGULAR base \( 0 \le x \le [[a]],\ 0 \le y \le [[b]] \), by evaluating \( \int_{0}^{[[a]]}\int_{0}^{[[b]]}\int_{0}^{[[p]]x+[[q]]y+[[r]]} 1\,dz\,dy\,dx \).
solution: The innermost integral gives the height \([[p]]x+[[q]]y+[[r]]\). Then \( \int_0^{[[a]]}\int_0^{[[b]]} \left([[p]]x+[[q]]y+[[r]]\right)dy\,dx = \dfrac{[[p]][[a]]^2[[b]]}{2} + \dfrac{[[q]][[a]][[b]]^2}{2} + [[r]][[a]][[b]] = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This uses only the constant term \([[r]]\) times the base area, forgetting the tilted \(px+qy\) part of the top surface.
This uses only the tilted part of the top surface, forgetting the constant \([[r]]\) contribution.
This is off by one from the correct value.
This is off by one from the correct value.
This is off by two from the correct value.

stem: Find the volume of the solid bounded below by \(z=0\), above by the paraboloid \( z = x^2+y^2+[[k]] \), over the RECTANGULAR base \( 0 \le x \le [[a]],\ 0 \le y \le [[b]] \), by evaluating \( \int_{0}^{[[a]]}\int_{0}^{[[b]]}\int_{0}^{x^2+y^2+[[k]]} 1\,dz\,dy\,dx \).
solution: The innermost integral gives the height \(x^2+y^2+[[k]]\). Then \( \int_0^{[[a]]}\int_0^{[[b]]}\left(x^2+y^2+[[k]]\right)dy\,dx = \dfrac{[[a]]^3[[b]]}{3} + \dfrac{[[a]][[b]]^3}{3} + [[k]][[a]][[b]] = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This drops the constant \([[k]]\) contribution to the height entirely.
This uses only the constant \([[k]]\) contribution, forgetting the curved \(x^2+y^2\) part of the top surface.
This is off by one from the correct value.
This is off by one from the correct value.
This is off by two from the correct value.

stem: Find the volume of the solid bounded below by \(z=0\), above by the plane \(z=[[c]]\), whose base is the PARALLELOGRAM \( 0 \le x \le [[a]],\ x \le y \le x + [[b]] \), by evaluating \( \int_{0}^{[[a]]}\int_{x}^{x+[[b]]}\int_{0}^{[[c]]} 1\,dz\,dy\,dx \).
solution: The innermost \(z\)-integral just gives the constant height \([[c]]\). Then \( \int_0^{[[a]]}\int_x^{x+[[b]]}[[c]]\,dy\,dx = \int_0^{[[a]]} [[c]][[b]]\,dx = [[c]][[b]][[a]] = [[answer]] \), the area of the parallelogram base (\([[a]]\times[[b]]\)) times the height \([[c]]\).
distractor_rationales: This halves the base area as if the region were a triangle rather than a parallelogram.
This uses \(\tfrac12[[a]]\) as the effective base width, mistaking the shape for a triangle scaled by \([[a]]\).
This is off by one from the correct value.
This is off by one from the correct value.
This is off by two from the correct value.
